commit.message:
feat: auto-update option for LXC installations

Add AUTO_UPDATE config toggle (off by default). When enabled on
LXC/systemd, the hourly update checker automatically runs upgrade.sh
after detecting a new version — no manual action needed.

- New config key AUTO_UPDATE in DEFAULT_CONFIG and allowed_keys
- Toggle in web UI under Updates section (standalone only)
- _should_auto_update() checks config + runtime (LXC only, not Docker/HA)
- _auto_apply_update() runs upgrade.sh in executor with 120s timeout
- /api/update/info returns auto_update status

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

commit.message:
fix: wrap all API errors in McpException with logging

Centralise error handling in OuraApiClient so all failure paths:
- Log to error.log via ILogger (visible via get_error_log tool)
- Throw McpException with user-friendly messages (no raw stack traces)
- Catch timeouts (TaskCanceledException) alongside HttpRequestException
- Strip query strings from URLs in user-facing messages

Error handling:
- Token retrieval/refresh failures -> authentication error
- Network errors and timeouts -> network error
- 401 -> refresh token, retry (split token vs network errors)
- 429 -> respect Retry-After, retry once
- 502/503/504 -> retry once, fall through on different error
- 403 -> subscription/access error
- Other HTTP errors -> generic API error
- Malformed JSON -> parse error
- Null/empty responses -> empty response error

15 new tests covering all error paths.

Fixes #27

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

commit.message:
fix(ha-addon): AppArmor enforce with blanket file/signal rules

Modeled after Music Assistant addon profile. Granular path rules
are unreliable on Docker overlayfs — use blanket file/signal access
and restrict via capabilities and network rules instead.

Also adds cd /app to entrypoint.sh for S6 cwd compatibility.

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

repository.name: sendspin-bt-bridge

commit.message:
fix: add cd /app to entrypoint.sh for S6 cwd compatibility

S6 with-contenv may reset cwd even when the run script does cd /app.
Making entrypoint.sh self-contained ensures it always runs from /app
regardless of how it's invoked.

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

commit.message:
fix(ha-addon): AppArmor enforce mode — rix for scripts, signal rules

Key fixes:
- ix → rix for /init, /s6-init, /bin/**, /usr/bin/**, /package/**,
  /command/** — shell scripts need read for shebang parsing
- Added signal (send) rule for S6 process management
- Added /dev/tty rw, /run/{,**} rwk, /etc/cont-finish.d/**
- /app/** rixm — allow memory-mapped Python .pyc files
- /tmp/** rwk — lock files need k (lock)
- Removed complain flag — profile is now in enforce mode
- Simplified /s6-init: removed runtime chmod (set at build time)

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

commit.message:
Update copilot instructions: fix stale refs, add config/objectives/metrics/diagnose docs (#168)

- Fix triforce.json → triforce.yaml references (3 occurrences)
- Fix train.py and evaluate.py CLI signatures to match actual usage
- Add Configuration Files, Objectives & Routing, Metrics, Multi-Head Models,
  Training Hints sections

container restart. The /s6-init wrapper re-applies chmod +x
before exec-ing /init.

commit.message:
feat: file-based error logging, log viewer tool, and empty data handling

Add Serilog file sink for persistent error logging to
~/.oura-mcp/logs/error.log with daily rolling, 7-day retention,
and structured format (timestamp, level, source context, message,
exception).

Add get_error_log MCP tool so AI assistants can self-diagnose tool
failures by reading the error log.

Fix collection endpoint handling to gracefully handle null/empty
data arrays — returns empty list instead of crashing.

Improve 403 error handling: log full API response at Error level
and throw McpException with friendly user-facing message.

Closes #24

commit.message:
Fix wavefront marking exit boundary tiles as distance 0 (#166)

When the objective is to exit the room (e.g., move north), the wavefront
was marking ALL boundary tiles as distance 0. This allowed the model to
idle at the boundary moving left/right with 0 PBRS rewards.

Fix: wavefront exit targets are now a single center tile one step
off-screen (e.g., center of north exit at y=-1). This creates a gradient
along the exit boundary — lateral movement away from center produces
negative PBRS, pushing the model to converge on the exit center and leave.

Also fixes dungeon exit reverse mappings in _get_exit_tiles to use the
actual boundary tile coordinates (not the door tile coordinates).

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

repository.name: sendspin-bt-bridge
repository.description: A Bluetooth bridge for Music Assistant — connects your Bluetooth speakers to the MA Sendspin protocol. Runs as a Docker container, a Home Assistant addon, or a native LXC container on Proxmox VE/OpenWrt

commit.message:
feat: universal version update checker with UI badge

- Add services/update_checker.py: background task polls GitHub releases API hourly
- Add /api/update/check, /api/update/info, /api/update/apply endpoints
- Add update badge in UI header (green pill linking to release notes)
- Include update_available in /api/status and SSE snapshots
- Start update checker as asyncio task in main()
- Fix lxc/upgrade.sh: add missing route modules and new files
- Platform-aware: LXC gets one-click upgrade, Docker/HA show instructions

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>
